title: Self-Aligning Toroidal Cable Clamp with Passive Strain Relief
background: Existing cable clamps for high-flex robotic assemblies either rely on rigid clamping surfaces that damage insulation under repeated cycling, or on soft elastomer grips that drift under load. Neither approach accommodates bend-radius variance in multi-conductor cables without introducing torsional stress at the anchor point.
inventive_elements: Toroidal clamp body with a compliant inner lumen that self-centers the cable
Passive strain-relief channel tangent to the cable entry axis
Dual-durometer elastomer insert with a harder outer shell and softer contact surface
Keyed mount that rotates within a defined angular range to absorb torsional load
embodiments: In one embodiment, the clamp body is formed from injection-molded polyamide and carries a silicone elastomer insert of Shore 40A hardness at the cable contact surface and Shore 70A at the outer load-bearing shell. The strain-relief channel subtends an arc of at least 30 degrees tangent to the cable entry axis, with a bend radius no less than four times the cable diameter. A keyed mounting boss permits ±15 degrees of rotation about the mount axis. In a further embodiment, the clamp includes a ferrule retention shelf that accepts an M4 through-bolt.
prior_art_summary: US 9,123,456 (Smith, 2015) discloses a split-shell cable clamp with elastomer lining but does not address torsional compliance at the anchor. US 10,234,567 (Chen, 2019) teaches a rotating cable passthrough but requires active bearings. Neither reference combines passive torsional compliance with dual-durometer contact.

title: Closed-Loop Neural Stimulation Device with Biomarker-Driven Adaptation
background: Implantable neural stimulators for chronic pain, essential tremor, and epilepsy currently deliver open-loop pulse trains with clinician-set parameters that drift out of optimal range as disease state evolves. Existing closed-loop systems react only to gross physiological signals (e.g., tremor accelerometry) and require centimeter-scale surface electrodes that miss focal network changes. Patient-specific stimulation tuning remains a manual, months-long titration process.
inventive_elements: Local field potential (LFP) sensing on at least four microelectrode contacts with coherence analysis across contacts
On-device biomarker classifier (embedded neural network) trained on patient-specific baseline recordings
Adaptive stimulation controller that modulates pulse amplitude, frequency, and contact selection within physician-defined safety envelopes
Event-triggered parameter logging to external programmer for supervised review without continuous telemetry
Fail-safe reversion to open-loop mode when classifier confidence drops below configurable threshold
embodiments: In one embodiment, the stimulator houses a 16-channel amplifier ASIC sampling at 2 kHz per channel, a low-power DSP running a 4-layer quantized neural network trained on 72 hours of baseline recordings per patient, and a constant-current stimulation engine with per-contact amplitude resolution of 10 microamps. The classifier emits a state estimate every 100 ms, driving the stimulation controller through a proportional-integral loop bounded by physician-defined limits (frequency 2-185 Hz, amplitude 0-8 mA). If classifier confidence falls below 0.7 over a 5-second window, the device reverts to the last validated open-loop parameter set and raises a flag on the next programmer session. In a further embodiment, logging is triggered only on state transitions to minimize memory and battery use.
prior_art_summary: US 11,234,567 (Medtronic, 2022) discloses a closed-loop spinal cord stimulator using evoked compound action potentials but requires continuous stimulation artifacts and cannot operate in sensing-only mode. US 10,987,654 (NeuroPace, 2021) teaches seizure detection via LFP but uses fixed templates rather than patient-specific learned classifiers. Neither reference combines per-contact coherence, on-device learning, and confidence-gated fail-safe reversion.

title: Citation-Verified Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Enterprise Q&A
background: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems fetch documents relevant to a user query and condition a language model on the retrieved text to produce grounded answers. Current RAG pipelines exhibit two failure modes in enterprise deployments: (1) the model cites documents that do not actually support the generated claim (hallucinated citations), and (2) the model omits citations for claims that ARE supported by retrieved content, forcing human reviewers to re-verify every output. Existing solutions rely on post-hoc string matching that is brittle to paraphrase or on expensive multi-step chain-of-thought prompting.
inventive_elements: Generation-time citation constraint that enforces each factual claim to include a span identifier at emission
Secondary verification model that computes semantic entailment between each claim and its cited span
Confidence-weighted rewrite loop that redrafts claims failing entailment above a threshold without re-triggering retrieval
Per-claim provenance graph persisted alongside the response for downstream audit
Optional expert-reviewed ground-truth feedback channel that updates the verification model's decision threshold per domain
embodiments: In one embodiment, the system receives a query, retrieves top-k passages from a vector index, and passes them to a generator model via a structured prompt that requires every sentence containing a factual claim to terminate with an inline span reference of the form [doc_id:offset_start-offset_end]. A verifier model — smaller than the generator — computes a natural-language inference score between each claim and its referenced span. Claims scoring below a domain-specific threshold (default 0.72 for regulated domains, 0.55 for general) are passed to a rewrite step that instructs the generator to restate the claim using only content present in already-retrieved passages. The final response is accompanied by a provenance graph serialized as JSON-LD, mapping each output token span to the supporting passage. In a further embodiment, reviewer feedback on published responses updates a per-domain threshold via a Bayesian posterior on reviewer rejection rate.
prior_art_summary: US 2023/0178923 (Microsoft, 2023) discloses RAG with citation but computes citation relevance via cosine similarity only, not semantic entailment. US 11,789,012 (Google, 2023) teaches verifier models for hallucination detection but lacks the constrained generation format and rewrite loop. Neither reference combines generation-time span constraints, entailment verification, and a persisted provenance graph.
